title: alchemilla hoppeana subsp. conjuncta
intro: Alchemilla hoppeana subsp. conjuncta, also known as a subspecies of the lady's mantle, is a perennial herb that belongs to the roser family (Rosaceae) and is native to mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, the plant is used for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues, including wounds, ulcers, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Alchemilla hoppeana subsp. conjuncta include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
